accuracy_overall.note: method='multi_seed_std' is the closest schema enum: here it is the descriptive across-FOLD std over the 3 leave-one-location-out folds (RM_142/RM_572C/RM_573C), not seeds -- NOT a 95% CI.

macro_f1.note: method='multi_seed_std' is the closest schema enum: here it is the descriptive across-FOLD std over the 3 leave-one-location-out folds (RM_142/RM_572C/RM_573C), not seeds -- NOT a 95% CI.

verification.note: Cross-room = the paper's SCENARIO-SPLIT (leave-one-location-out cross-validation): the corrected tprime (official recipe lr=2e-4, batch 128) is trained on all locations but one and tested on the held-out location IN FULL (unseen in training), for each of the 3 locations. accuracy_overall is the CV MEAN over the 3 folds (per-fold, held-out: 142 0.9642; 572C 0.9841; 573C 0.8922). Reproduces the paper's finding that the scenario-split is HARDER than within-distribution (0.947 mean cross-room vs 0.995 within-distribution closed_set) and that RM_573C is the most challenging location (0.892). PER-WINDOW on our recording-level location splits (no window leaks across the location boundary). Fold splits: proto-tprime-wifi4-crossroom-heldout-142-v1 (sha256:ae084f9cd0d63...), proto-tprime-wifi4-crossroom-heldout-572c-v1 (sha256:ee9c84d2f147c...), proto-tprime-wifi4-crossroom-heldout-573c-v1 (sha256:ef4e82346f898...). self_reported (a faithful in-repo run, not maintainer-verified).
